rationale: The citation goes beyond a generic background list because the citing paper attributes a specific substantive finding to Van Heerde et al. (2013): lower long-run advertising elasticities in recessions and an implied recommendation to cut advertising. The authors use that finding in their limitations/future-research discussion to frame competing views on recession-period marketing actions. Although it is not central to their structural model of alcohol consumption, it plays a meaningful positioning and interpretive role in how they discuss supply-side strategy over business cycles.
relation_description: The citing paper uses Van Heerde et al. (2013) as specific prior evidence on how advertising effectiveness changes over the business cycle. It leverages that result to contrast alternative managerial recommendations and motivate future work that incorporates firms' strategic marketing responses during recessions.
